answers.answer: D) Flag the contradiction: You mentioned snake_case in turn 3, but the existing codebase uses camelCase and you asked me to follow existing style. Which should I prioritize for this project? Reason: When facing contradictory instructions, the best approach is to explicitly identify the conflict and request clarification rather than assuming which instruction takes priority. This demonstrates accurate memory of context, ability to recognize contradictions, and proactive communication.

Current Schema (Version 8): { id, first_name, last_name, full_name (computed), role (admin|editor|viewer), avatar_url, preferences {theme, language}, notifications_enabled (boolean, top-level), email (nullable, only for admin or own profile), last_login_at (ISO), created_at (ISO), status (active|suspended|deactivated) }. Changelog: V1-initial {id,name,email,created_at}; V2-add role, name->display_name; V3-split display_name to first_name+last_name, add computed full_name; V4-add avatar_url and preferences object; V5-email removed from default, added as nullable field; V6-add last_login_at, created_at changed to Unix; V7-reverted created_at back to ISO; V8-add status, moved notifications_enabled to top-level boolean.
